item.title: Shootings, Labor Day, Teacher Union
item.ns0:title: Young Father Gunned Down Near His Home in Bain Town Shooting — Saturday, June 6th, 2026
item.description:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, communities in New Providence and Grand Bahama are reeling following a series of fatal shootings that have left two families in mourning. Tensions reach a boiling point in the education sector as the Bahamas Union of Teachers responds to a "shot over the bow" regarding government salary promises, while immigration officials ramp up enforcement with dozens of deportations. We also join the nation in remembering the victims of a tragic holiday accident and provide an exclusive look at the growing partnerships surrounding the multi-million dollar Royal Beach Club project. Plus, stay tuned for highlights from the baseball diamond and your complete weekend weather forecast as the first week of hurricane season continues.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, communities in New Providence and Grand Bahama are reeling following a series of fatal shootings that have left two families in mourning. Tensions reach a boiling point in the education sector as the Bahamas Union of Teachers responds to a "shot over the bow" regarding government salary promises, while immigration officials ramp up enforcement with dozens of deportations. We also join the nation in remembering the victims of a tragic holiday accident and provide an exclusive look at the growing partnerships surrounding the multi-million dollar Royal Beach Club project. Plus, stay tuned for highlights from the baseball diamond and your complete weekend weather forecast as the first week of hurricane season continues.
-----

item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, communities in New Providence and Grand Bahama are reeling following a series of fatal shootings that have left two families in mourning. Tensions reach a boiling point in the education sector as the Bahamas Union of Teachers responds to a "shot over the bow" regarding government salary promises, while immigration officials ramp up enforcement with dozens of deportations. We also join the nation in remembering the victims of a tragic holiday accident and provide an exclusive look at the growing partnerships surrounding the multi-million dollar Royal Beach Club project. Plus, stay tuned for highlights from the baseball diamond and your complete weekend weather forecast as the first week of hurricane season continues.

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
